c74object.name: dk.regressorcreate~
c74object.digest: Create a regressor based on training data
c74object.description: The dk.regressorcreate~ object will create a regressor based on incoming pairs of data, be it descriptors, synth parameters, or any arbitrary data, as well as parallel audio analysis of any incoming synth sounds.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithm have a look at this article:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/mlpregressor

inlet.description: Incoming buffer~ of descriptors to attach to the current label/class. Also takes the messages for object control.

outlet.description: The nearest match in the pre-trained classifier.
Total amount of entries that have been sent to the classifier.
Training loss value for the neural network.

method.name: addpoint
method.digest: Add a point to the regressor
method.description: Add a point tot he regressor.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print regressor contents to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the regressor to the Max console.

method.name: train
method.description: Enables the training mode for the internal regressor.

method.name: write
method.description: Write a .json file with the regressor and all related meta-data to disk. to disk.

attribute.name: activation
attribute.description:
An integer indicating which activation function each neuron in the output layer will use. Changing this will reset the neural network, clearing any learning that has happened. The options are:
Possible values:
0 = identity (the output range can be any value)
1 = sigmoid (the output will always always be greater than 0 and less than 1)
2 = relu (the output will always be greater than or equal to 0)
3 = tanh (the output will always be greater than -1 and less than 1)

attribute.name: batchsize
attribute.description: The number of data points to use in between adjustments of the MLP's internal parameters during training.

attribute.name: hiddenlayers
attribute.description: An array of numbers that specifies the internal structure of the neural network. Each number in the list represents one hidden layer of the neural network, the value of which is the number of neurons in that layer. Changing this will reset the neural network, clearing any learning that has happened.

attribute.name: learnrate
attribute.description: A scalar for indicating how much the neural network should adjust its internal parameters during training. This is the most important parameter to adjust while training a neural network.

attribute.name: maxiter
attribute.description: The number of epochs to train for when fit is called on the object. An epoch consists of training on all the data points one time.

attribute.name: momentum
attribute.description: A scalar that applies a portion of previous adjustments to a current adjustment being made by the neural network during training.

attribute.name: outputactivation
attribute.description: An integer indicating which activation function each neuron in the output layer will use. Options are the same as activation. Changing this will reset the neural network, clearing any learning that has happened.

attribute.name: validation
attribute.description: A percentage (represented as a decimal) of the data points to randomly select, set aside, and not use for training (this "validation set" is reselected on each fit). These points will be used after each epoch to check how the neural network is performing. If it is found to be no longer improving, training will stop, even if a fit has not reached its maxiter number of epochs.
